title: Curry Meatballs
excerpt: Ingredients 60 ml oil 1 onion, chopped 10 ml coarsely crushed cumin seeds (jeera) 2 garlic cloves, finely chopped 10 ml grated, fresh ginger root 30 ml curry powder of your choice 10 ml turmeric 5 ml dried chilli flakes 60 ml vinegar 30 ml sugar 1 tin tomato and onion mix salt and pepper to …
html:
Ingredients

  • 60 ml oil
  • 1 onion, chopped
  • 10 ml coarsely crushed cumin seeds (jeera)
  • 2 garlic cloves, finely chopped
  • 10 ml grated, fresh ginger root
  • 30 ml curry powder of your choice
  • 10 ml turmeric
  • 5 ml dried chilli flakes
  • 60 ml vinegar
  • 30 ml sugar
  • 1 tin tomato and onion mix
  • salt and pepper to taste
  • a splash of water, if needed
  • 600 g good quality boerewors, skin removed
  • a handful of coarsely chopped coriander leaves or herbs of your choice

Method

Heat the oil in a frying pan and fry the onion and cumin seeds until the onion is glassy.

Stir in the garlic, ginger, curry powder, turmeric and chilli flakes and fry, stirring, until fragrant, about a minute.

Stir in the sugar and vinegar and then add the tomato and onion mix.

Season to taste, stir well and let simmer gently for 5 minutes. If the sauce gets too thick, add a splash of water.

Roll medium-sized meatballs from the sausage meat and place them in a single layer in an oven dish – don't use too large a dish; the meatballs should fit in snugly.

Pour the curry sauce over and bake at 180°C for 20 minutes or until the meat is done to your liking.

Remove from the oven, sprinkle herbs over and serve.

Makes 4 servings with rice or mashed potatoes.

Easy!

Tip: This sauce is also lovely with pap and braaivleis.
